behaviors.description: Speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels for attendee attribution
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is whether the real speech-to-text API actually returns speaker labels in its response. A mock API can always return them, hiding the case where the real API omits them or structures them differently. We need to call the actual speech-to-text service to verify labels are present and correctly extracted.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real speech-to-text API with a meeting recording containing multiple speakers, verify the response includes speaker labels, and verify the service correctly extracts and passes these labels to the summarization step.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: This test would fail at Integration level if we mocked the API, since the mock would always return labels even if the real API doesn't.

behaviors.description: Summary contains at least one action item for every attendee assigned a task
behaviors.justification: Whether the LLM reliably extracts and attributes one action item per assigned attendee depends on the real model's non-deterministic output. Mocking the LLM to always return perfect action items masks the actual failure: the real LLM might miss attendees, misattribute, or produce incomplete action lists. This is fundamentally an LLM capability boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real LLM with a transcript containing explicit task assignments to 3+ attendees, verify the structured output contains at least one action item for each assigned attendee with correct attribution.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: An Integration-level test with a mocked LLM would pass even if the real LLM fails to extract actions consistently.

behaviors.description: Key decisions appear in decisions section, not buried in narrative
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on whether the real LLM can reliably identify and categorize decisions into the structured field rather than leaving them in free-form narrative. Mocking the LLM to perfectly categorize decisions hides the real failure: the LLM may conflate decisions with narrative text or miss subtle decisions entirely.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real LLM with a transcript containing 2+ explicit decisions (e.g., 'we decided to launch in Q2', 'decided to hire two engineers'), verify each appears in the decisions section of the output, not buried in narrative text.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: A mocked LLM at Integration level would always correctly categorize decisions, hiding LLM categorization failures.

behaviors.description: Long audio (>60 min) is segmented, processed sequentially, and merged into coherent summary
behaviors.justification: While splitting logic (Unit/Integration) and sequential processing (Integration) are deterministic, the critical failure is whether the merged summary from multiple LLM-summarized segments is coherent, complete, and free of contradictions. This depends on the real LLM's ability to merge segment summaries intelligently. Mocking the LLM hides whether real merges are lossy or contradictory.
behaviors.test_description: Upload a real 90-minute meeting recording (or synthesized from two 45-minute segments with linked context), verify the service segments it correctly, call the real LLM to summarize each segment and merge, verify the merged summary is coherent (no contradictions), contains all key decisions and action items, and loses no critical information.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: An Integration test with mocked LLM could verify splitting and sequential structure, but would not catch incoherent merges or information loss that the real LLM produces.

behaviors.description: Full pipeline completes end-to-end without manual intervention for a real meeting recording
behaviors.justification: The full pipeline chains real speech-to-text API (System) and real LLM summarization (Agentic). The LLM step is the binding constraint because mocking it would hide failures in the real model's ability to produce valid structured output. While the speech-to-text API call is a System dependency, the agentic step (LLM correctness) determines whether the output is actually useful.
behaviors.test_description: Upload a real meeting recording (or high-fidelity test recording), run the complete pipeline with real speech-to-text API and real LLM, verify structured output (action items, decisions, attendee attributions) is delivered, contains no errors, and requires zero manual corrections.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: A System-level test with a real speech-to-text API but mocked LLM would not catch failures where the real LLM produces malformed or incomplete structured output.
